sections.label: Description (paste into Google Business Profile)
sections.content:
Just a stone's throw from the Mississippi River, the Big Muddy Inn & Blues Room is a one-of-a-kind boutique hotel and live music venue in the heart of historic downtown Natchez. Our 6,000-square-foot property features six art-filled suites, each meticulously designed to honor a legendary blues figure. From the Delta-inspired Muddy Waters Suite to the British Invasion rooms, every corner of the inn celebrates the music and culture that shaped the South.

Guests can enjoy live performances in our intimate, 50-seat Blues Room, relax with a cigar in the private courtyard, or explore the fine art collection curated throughout the inn.

sections.label: Q1: Parking
sections.content:
Q: Is parking available at The Big Muddy Inn?
A: Yes! Free parking is included with your stay. We have convenient off-street parking available for all guests. Downtown Natchez is also very walkable, so you can leave the car and explore on foot once you've arrived. If you have any questions about parking when you arrive, just call us at (769) 376-8045 and we'll point you right to it.

sections.label: Q2: Check-in/Check-out
sections.content:
Q: What time is check-in and check-out?
A: Check-in is at 3:00 PM and check-out is at 11:00 AM. If you need an early check-in or late check-out, please reach out to us in advance at info@thebigmuddyinn.com or call (769) 376-8045 — we'll do our best to accommodate you based on availability. We want your arrival and departure to be as smooth as possible.

sections.label: Q3: Breakfast
sections.content:
Q: Is breakfast included in the room rate?
A: Yes! Every guest receives a complimentary Southern breakfast each morning. We believe mornings should start right — especially in Natchez. Breakfast is served on-site, and it's the perfect way to fuel up before a day exploring our beautiful historic city. No need to hunt for a coffee shop; we've got you covered from the first meal of the day.

sections.label: Q4: Blues Room / Live music
sections.content:
Q: What is The Blues Room, and when is there live music?
A: The Blues Room is our intimate 50-seat speakeasy-style music venue located right inside the Inn. It's one of the only places in the country where you can walk downstairs in your pajamas and catch world-class live blues. We host live performances Thursday, Friday, and Saturday evenings. Our artist-in-residence, Amy Allen, performs regularly alongside a rotating lineup of talented musicians. Guests always have front-row access.

sections.label: Q5: Pet policy
sections.content:
Q: Are pets allowed at The Big Muddy Inn?
A: We love animals, but we are not able to accommodate pets at The Big Muddy Inn. To ensure the comfort and experience of all our guests — and to protect our carefully curated art-filled suites — we maintain a strict no-pet policy. If you're traveling with a service animal, please contact us directly at info@thebigmuddyinn.com before booking so we can discuss accommodations.

sections.label: Q6: Suite recommendations
sections.content:
Q: Which suite should I book for a special occasion like an anniversary or birthday?
A: All six of our suites are special, but for a truly unforgettable occasion, we recommend the Muddy Waters or B.B. King Suite — both are spacious, richly appointed, and feel like stepping into a piece of music history. If your partner loves a bit of mystery, the Robert Johnson Suite has a legendary mystique all its own. Call us at (769) 376-8045 and we'll help you choose the perfect room for your celebration.

sections.label: Q7: Nearby attractions
sections.content:
Q: How far is the Inn from downtown restaurants and attractions?
A: We are right in the heart of downtown Natchez — you are already there. The Mississippi River bluff is a 3-minute walk. Natchez Under-the-Hill, with its restaurants and bars, is 5 minutes on foot. Rosalie Mansion is 7 minutes away. Stanton Hall and the city's finest antebellum homes are all within a half-mile. Most guests never need their car during their stay.

sections.label: Q8: Adults only policy
sections.content:
Q: Is The Big Muddy Inn adults only? Are children allowed?
A: Yes, The Big Muddy Inn & Blues Room is an adults-only property. All guests must be 21 years of age or older. This policy allows us to maintain the sophisticated, relaxed atmosphere our guests love — and ensures everyone can fully enjoy The Blues Room experience. We hope you understand, and we look forward to welcoming you!

sections.label: Q9: Private events
sections.content:
Q: Can The Blues Room be reserved for a private event?
A: Absolutely! The Blues Room is available for private events — corporate gatherings, rehearsal dinners, birthday celebrations, and more. With 50 seats, silk-upholstered walls, velvet couches, and curated fine art, it is one of the most dramatic private event spaces in Natchez. We can arrange custom music and catering options. Contact us at info@thebigmuddyinn.com or call (769) 376-8045 to start planning.

sections.label: Q10: Things to do nearby
sections.content:
Q: What is there to do near The Big Muddy Inn in Natchez?
A: Natchez is one of the South's great undiscovered treasures. From our front door you can walk to antebellum mansions (Rosalie, Stanton Hall, Longwood), the Mississippi River bluff for breathtaking sunsets, the Under-the-Hill restaurant and bar district, and the Natchez Museum of African American History. The Natchez Trace Parkway is minutes away. And of course, The Blues Room is right downstairs — some nights, you won't need to go anywhere at all.

google_review_responses.title: Google Review Responses — Ready to Post

sections.label: Zachary Shrift ★★★★★
sections.content: Thank you so much, Zachary! We're thrilled you loved your stay and felt right at home. It means the world to us when guests appreciate the history and the music — that's what The Big Muddy is all about. We can't wait to welcome you back. Your next visit is going to be even better!

sections.label: Tom Clark ★★★★★
sections.content: Tom, thank you for such a kind review! We put a lot of thought into the details — especially those linens — so it's wonderful to hear they made your stay special. Natchez has so many gems to discover, and we'd love to help you find even more on your next trip. Hope to see you again soon!

sections.label: Leslee Sandifer ★★★★★
sections.content: Leslee, what an honor to be part of your 39th anniversary celebration! The image of your husband playing guitar and harmonica with Amy on the patio — that's exactly the kind of magic we hoped this place would create. Happy anniversary to you both, and we'd love to help you celebrate your 40th!

sections.label: Anna Ellis ★★★★★
sections.content: Anna and John, thank you! "Luxury with a casual friendly vibe" is exactly what we're going for, so that made our day. So glad you caught one of Amy's blues shows — those nights in The Blues Room are something special. We always love welcoming our New Orleans neighbors. See y'all soon!

sections.label: Michael Gray ★★★★★
sections.content: Michael, we're blushing! "The only home to stay when visiting Natchez" — we might just put that on a plaque. Thank you for recognizing all the things that make The Big Muddy unique. The music, the location, the hospitality — it all comes from the heart. We hope to welcome you back soon.

sections.label: Alex Pence ★★★★★
sections.content: Alex, 100 stars — we'll take it! It was such a joy to be part of your wedding celebration. Every suite has its own personality, and hosting a wedding group that gets to experience them all is one of our favorite things. Thank you for trusting us with such a special occasion. Congratulations again!

sections.label: Michele Anthony ★★★★★
sections.content: Michele, thank you! We're so glad you loved the musical theming and those dreamy beds. Natchez really is a foodie's paradise, and there's always something new to discover downtown. We'd love to have you back for another concert in the music hall — and maybe another stroll to your favorite restaurant!

sections.label: Zenarchy ★★★★★
sections.content: Thank you so much! The blues happy hours are one of our favorite traditions — there's nothing like live music with good company. We'd love to have you back for a full stay next time. The suites are just as special as The Blues Room!

sections.label: Andrea Brooks ★★★★★
sections.content: Andrea, "love at first sight" is the best compliment we could ask for! We worked hard to honor the history of this building while making it feel fresh and modern. So glad that came through for you. We miss you already — come back anytime!

sections.label: Rosemary Hallmark ★★★★★
sections.content: Rosemary, thank you for calling us a jewel! The music and the people really do make this place shine. We're grateful for guests like you who make every evening so much fun. Hope to see you back in Natchez soon!

sections.label: 5-Star — General
sections.content: [NAME], thank you so much for your wonderful review! We're thrilled you enjoyed [SPECIFIC THING THEY MENTIONED]. It's guests like you who make what we do so rewarding. We'd love to welcome you back to The Big Muddy anytime!

sections.label: 5-Star — Music Focused
sections.content: [NAME], thank you! So glad you caught [a show in The Blues Room / the live music]. Those nights are what The Big Muddy is all about — great music, great company, and a little Mississippi magic. We've got more shows coming up and would love to have you back!

sections.label: 5-Star — Special Occasion
sections.content: [NAME], what an honor to be part of your [anniversary / celebration / special trip]! Moments like those are exactly why we created this place. Thank you for sharing them with us, and we hope to see you back for the next milestone!

sections.label: 4-Star — Minor Issue
sections.content: [NAME], thank you for staying with us and for your thoughtful feedback! We're so glad you enjoyed [POSITIVE THING]. We appreciate you mentioning [ISSUE] — we're always looking for ways to improve. We'd love another chance to give you a perfect 5-star stay!

sections.label: 3-Star — Mixed
sections.content: [NAME], thank you for your honest review. We're glad [POSITIVE ASPECT] met your expectations, and we hear you on [CONCERN]. That's not the experience we want for our guests. Please reach out to us at info@thebigmuddyinn.com.

sections.label: 1-2 Star — Negative
sections.content: [NAME], we're sorry to hear your stay didn't meet expectations. We take feedback seriously and would love the opportunity to learn more. Please reach out at info@thebigmuddyinn.com or (769) 376-8045 so we can address this directly.

sections.label: Short Description (255 chars max)
sections.content: A boutique blues inn in the heart of historic Natchez, MS. Six uniquely designed suites, a 50-seat live music venue, and a cigar courtyard. Southern hospitality with a soundtrack. Book your stay at thebigmuddyinn.com.

sections.label: Long Description / Our Story
sections.content:
Welcome to The Big Muddy Inn & Blues Room — where the spirit of the Mississippi River meets the soul of Southern hospitality.

Nestled at 411 North Commerce Street in historic downtown Natchez, Mississippi, The Big Muddy Inn offers six thoughtfully appointed, blues-themed suites, each one a tribute to the musical heritage that flows through this storied city.

Our Blues Room is a 50-seat live music venue where the best local and regional talent takes the stage. Step outside and you'll find our cigar courtyard — a quiet, beautifully curated outdoor space perfect for unwinding.

We're proud to be part of the Natchez community, and we can't wait to share it with you.

In the meantime, you're welcome to:

We love what we do here in Natchez, and we can't wait to share it with you. Sweet dreams — and we'll talk soon!

Warmly,
Tracy & The Big Muddy Inn Team

sections.label: Listing Title
sections.content: B.B. King Suite at The Big Muddy Inn — Boutique Blues Hotel

sections.label: Description
sections.content:
Named for the King of the Blues himself, the B.B. King Suite is a celebration of the man who brought the blues to the world. Vibrant colors, regal furnishings, and an extensive art collection make this our most celebrated suite.

Your stay includes access to our private cigar courtyard and the chance to enjoy live performances in The Blues Room — our intimate, 50-seat music venue right in the inn.

We're located in the heart of downtown Natchez, within walking distance of restaurants, the river bluffs, and historic pilgrimage homes.

sections.label: Property Description (paste into main description field)
sections.content:
Welcome to The Big Muddy Inn & Blues Room — a one-of-a-kind boutique inn where Delta blues history, bold design, and live music share the same roof.

Set in a beautifully renovated 6,000 sq ft historic building in the heart of downtown Natchez, the Inn offers six elegantly appointed suites, each named for a blues legend: Muddy Waters, John Lee Hooker, Robert Johnson, B.B. King, British Invasion I, and British Invasion II. Every room is a curated work of art — playful patterns, luxe fabrics, and original fine art as colorful as the icons who inspired them.

Downstairs, The Blues Room is an intimate, 50-seat speakeasy-style music venue with velvet couches, silk-upholstered walls, and live performances every Thursday, Saturday, and Sunday. It is the only inn in Mississippi where you can fall asleep to live blues music.

A complimentary Southern breakfast is served each morning. Free parking and free Wi-Fi are included. We are two blocks from the Mississippi River and walking distance to Natchez's finest antebellum mansions, restaurants, and the famous Under-the-Hill district.

Adults only. Minimum age 21+. No pets.

The Big Muddy is not just a place to sleep — it is an experience you will talk about for years.

sections.label: Short Description / Tagline (150 chars)
sections.content: Boutique blues inn in historic Natchez. 6 suites named for legends. Live music Thu–Sat in our 50-seat speakeasy. Southern breakfast included.

sections.label: Muddy Waters Suite
sections.content: Named for the father of Chicago blues, McKinley Morganfield — known to the world as Muddy Waters — this suite pays homage to the man who electrified the Delta sound and inspired generations of musicians. Rich, deep tones and bold art set the mood. A masterfully appointed retreat for anyone who appreciates the roots of American music.

sections.label: John Lee Hooker Suite
sections.content: The John Lee Hooker Suite honors one of the most influential figures in blues history, whose driving boogie rhythms changed music forever. Expect an immersive, art-forward space layered with pattern and personality — a room with the same infectious energy as the man himself. One night here and you'll understand why John Lee Hooker's music still feels alive.

sections.label: Robert Johnson Suite
sections.content: Legend has it Robert Johnson sold his soul at the crossroads for the blues. This suite is steeped in that mystique — moody, magnetic, and unforgettable. Named for the Delta bluesman who influenced everyone from Eric Clapton to The Rolling Stones, the Robert Johnson Suite is the most storied room in the house. Book it if you dare.

sections.label: B.B. King Suite
sections.content: Riley B. King — B.B. — was the King of Blues for a reason. This suite matches his larger-than-life presence with an elegantly appointed space full of warmth, color, and fine art. Named for the Mississippi-born legend who made Lucille sing, the B.B. King Suite is ideal for guests who appreciate the regal side of the blues tradition.

sections.label: British Invasion I
sections.content: When the British Invasion brought American blues back to its hometown in the 1960s, something magical happened. This suite celebrates that moment — the Rolling Stones, The Animals, and the transatlantic love affair with the Delta sound. Bold design, a nod to mod sensibility, and a flair for the dramatic make this one of the Inn's most distinctive rooms.

sections.label: British Invasion II
sections.content: A companion to British Invasion I, this suite continues the celebration of the British bands who carried Mississippi's music to the world. Equally vibrant and thoughtfully designed, British Invasion II suits guests who love the moment when two musical cultures collided and created something timeless. Ideal for a second room when traveling with friends.

sections.label: Amenity Checklist
sections.content:
GENERAL: Free Wi-Fi (all rooms + public areas), Free parking (on-site), Non-smoking rooms, Air conditioning, Heating, Daily housekeeping

FOOD & DRINK: Breakfast included (American/Southern), Complimentary breakfast

ENTERTAINMENT: Live music/entertainment, Lounge/bar on site (The Blues Room)

PROPERTY: Courtyard/outdoor space, Historic building, Luggage storage

ACCESS: 24-hour front desk (or closest option)

ROOM AMENITIES: Private bathroom, Wardrobe/closet

sections.label: House Rules (paste into House Rules section)
sections.content:
CHECK-IN: 3:00 PM – 9:00 PM
CHECK-OUT: 11:00 AM
Early check-in and late check-out may be available upon request, subject to availability.

AGE RESTRICTION: Adults only. All guests must be 21 years of age or older.

PETS: No pets permitted.

SMOKING: Non-smoking property. Smoking permitted in the outdoor courtyard only.

QUIET HOURS: The Blues Room hosts live music Thursday, Friday, and Saturday evenings. Guests who book The Big Muddy Inn are booking into a live music venue — this is part of the experience. Out of respect for neighbors and guests, all amplified music concludes by 11:00 PM.

CANCELLATION: Free cancellation up to 48 hours before check-in.

PAYMENTS: Select your accepted payment methods during setup.

sections.label: Nearby Attractions
sections.content:
• Natchez Bluff & Mississippi River Overlook — 0.2 miles (3-minute walk). Stunning sunset views over the Mississippi River.
• Natchez Under-the-Hill — 0.3 miles (5-minute walk). Historic riverfront district with restaurants, bars, and the legendary Under-the-Hill Saloon.
• Rosalie Mansion — 0.4 miles (7-minute walk). 1823 Federal-style antebellum mansion; site of the annual Natchez Balloon Festival.
• Stanton Hall — 0.5 miles (8-minute walk). One of the most magnificent Greek Revival mansions in America.
• Longwood — 1.2 miles (5-minute drive). The largest octagonal house in the United States, a National Historic Landmark.
• Natchez Museum of African American History and Culture — 0.6 miles (10-minute walk).
• Natchez Brewing Company — 0.4 miles (7-minute walk). Local craft brewery with Neapolitan pizza.

sections.label: What Makes This Property Special
sections.content:
The Big Muddy Inn & Blues Room is the only place in Mississippi — possibly in America — where you can sleep in a suite named for a blues legend and then walk downstairs to hear live music in an intimate speakeasy on the same night.

Every one of our six suites is a unique work of art, designed to honor the blues icons who gave Mississippi its musical soul: Muddy Waters, Robert Johnson, John Lee Hooker, B.B. King, and the British bands they inspired. No two rooms are alike.

Our Blues Room — a 50-seat velvet-and-silk speakeasy — hosts live performances Thursday, Friday, and Saturday nights. Our artist-in-residence, Amy Allen, performs regularly alongside visiting musicians from across the South.

We serve a complimentary Southern breakfast every morning. Downtown Natchez is walkable from our front door. Free parking is included.

This is not a chain hotel. It is a destination.

sections.label: Property Description (paste into Expedia)
sections.content:
The Big Muddy Inn & Blues Room is Natchez's most distinctive boutique inn — a 6,000 sq ft historic landmark in the heart of downtown where blues history lives on every wall.

Choose from six individually designed suites, each named for a blues icon: Muddy Waters, John Lee Hooker, Robert Johnson, B.B. King, British Invasion I, and British Invasion II. Art-filled, sumptuously appointed, and unlike any hotel room you have ever booked.

Every evening Thursday through Saturday, The Blues Room — our 50-seat, speakeasy-style music venue on the ground floor — comes alive with live performances. Guests enjoy front-row access to some of the South's most talented musicians without ever leaving the building.

Your stay includes a complimentary Southern breakfast each morning, free Wi-Fi, and free parking. We are two blocks from the Mississippi River and minutes from Natchez's legendary antebellum mansions, riverfront dining, and the historic Under-the-Hill district.

Adults only (21+). No pets.

With just 50 seats, every performance delivers a front-row experience to Mississippi's living musical heritage — blues, roots, Americana, jazz, and Southern soul.

sections.label: Competitor Reference
sections.content:
Monmouth Historic Inn: $323/night weekends
Dunleith: $179–$300/night weekends
The Guest House: $186/night weekends
Devereaux Shields: $239/night weekends
Natchez Grand Hotel: $150/night weeknights

At $169 weekends / $139 weeknights, we're the value play while still competitive.

sections.label: How to Update in CloudBeds
sections.content:
1. Log in to myfrontdesk.cloudbeds.com
2. Go to Rates & Availability (or Rate Calendar)
3. Select the date range
4. Set the new nightly rate for ALL room types
5. Save

Total time: ~10 minutes for all 4 batches.

yelp_category_fix.title: Yelp Category Fix — Step by Step

sections.label: Current Problem
sections.content:
The listing is categorized generically. Correct categories:
• Primary: Bed & Breakfast (under Hotels & Travel)
• Secondary: Music Venues (under Arts & Entertainment / Nightlife)

These are exact Yelp category names.

sections.label: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Log in at biz.yelp.com with tracy@thebigmuddyinn.com
2. If unclaimed: go to yelp.com/biz/the-big-muddy-inn-and-blues-room-natchez, click 'Claim this business', verify via phone call to (769) 376-8045
3. Click Business Information in left menu
4. Scroll to Category section, click edit
5. Type 'Bed & Breakfast' → select it as primary
6. Click 'Add another category' → type 'Music Venues' → select it
7. Click Save Changes

Changes go live within a few hours.

sections.label: Subject Line Option A
sections.content: The sky's going up — and so are our rates. Book now for Balloon Festival weekend.

sections.label: Subject Line Option B
sections.content: 6 suites. 3 days. 40 hot-air balloons. Natchez Balloon Festival, Oct 16–18.

sections.label: Subject Line Option C
sections.content: You've been here before. Now come back for the best weekend of the year.

sections.label: Email Body (paste into email platform)
sections.content:
Hey [First Name],

Thank you for being a Big Muddy guest. We're reaching out because something special is happening this October — and you deserve first access.

The Natchez Balloon Festival returns October 16–18, 2026, and it's the most magical weekend of the year in our little corner of Mississippi. Dozens of hot-air balloons drift over the Mississippi River at sunrise, the city fills with music and energy, and Natchez transforms into something you have to see to believe.

Here's the honest truth: Natchez hotels sell out completely for this weekend. Every year.

We only have 6 suites, and we're holding them at $275/night for Balloon Festival weekend. The Blues Room will have special programming all three nights. Breakfast is included. Parking is free.

If you've been thinking about coming back — this is the weekend to do it.

Book direct for the best rate:
thebigmuddyinn.com | (769) 376-8045

Don't wait on this one.

With Southern hospitality,
Tracy & The Big Muddy Inn Team

P.S. — Kids sleep free... just kidding. We're adults only. Tell your friends.

balloon_festival_google_event.title: Balloon Festival Google Business Event Post

sections.label: Event Title (headline field)
sections.content: Natchez Balloon Festival Weekend — Oct 16–18 | Special Rates at The Big Muddy

sections.label: Event Body Copy
sections.content:
The skies above Natchez are about to fill with hot-air balloons — and there's no better place to watch it happen than from a suite at The Big Muddy Inn & Blues Room.

The 41st Natchez Balloon Festival runs October 16–18, 2026, on the grounds of Rosalie Mansion — just four blocks from our front door. Dozens of balloons launch at sunrise and drift over the Mississippi River. There's a balloon glow Friday night, fireworks, and live music all weekend long.

We're offering a special Balloon Festival rate of $275/night for this weekend. That includes complimentary Southern breakfast each morning, free parking, free Wi-Fi, and nightly live music in The Blues Room.

We only have 6 suites. Natchez sells out completely for Balloon Festival weekend — every year, without exception.

Book direct now:
Phone: (769) 376-8045
Web: thebigmuddyinn.com
Email: info@thebigmuddyinn.com

Adults 21+ only. No pets.

sections.label: Post Settings
sections.content:
Event start date: October 16, 2026
Event end date: October 18, 2026
Post type: Event (not 'Update' or 'Offer')
CTA button: 'Book' or 'Learn more' → thebigmuddyinn.com
Optional offer code: BALLOON2026

Photo suggestion: Blues Room lit up at night, or aerial shot of balloons over Natchez bluff. JPEG or PNG, min 720x540px.
